meta.title: Lesson 16: 荷物を送りたいんですが — Grammar
meta.subject: japanese
meta.subtopic: I'd Like to Send Some Luggage
meta.description: Grammar patterns for Minna no Nihongo Lesson 16: I'd Like to Send Some Luggage

items.back:
Verb stem + たい = want to do ~

食べたい = want to eat
行きたい = want to go
見たい = want to see

Polite: 〜たいです
Negative: 〜たくないです
Past: 〜たかったです

〜たい + と思っています = am thinking of ~ing / I'm planning to ~

日本語を勉強したいと思っています。
= I'm thinking of studying Japanese.

More tentative than たいです. Often used for future plans.

〜んですが = It's that ~ (giving background before a request)

荷物を送りたいんですが...
= I'd like to send luggage... (so could you help?)

Implies that what follows is a request or question.
Very natural and polite in Japanese!

items.question: にほんへ行き ___ です。(I want to go to Japan.)
items.explanation: Verb stem + たい = want to do. 行く → 行き + たい = want to go.

items.question: にほんごをべんきょうし ___ と思っています。(I'm thinking of studying Japanese.)
items.explanation: 〜たいと思っています = am thinking of ~ing. たい + と思っています.

items.question: てがみをおく ___ んですが。(It's that I'd like to send a letter.)
items.explanation: 送る + たい = 送りたい. + んですが adds context/background before a request.

items.question: 〜んですが is used to:
items.answer: Give context before making a request
options: Give context before making a request
Give a reason with から
items.explanation: 〜んですが sets up background information, implying a request follows. Very natural in service situations.

items.question: I don't want to go. Which is correct?
options: 行きたいじゃないです
items.explanation: Negative of たい: たい → たくない + です. 行きたくないです = don't want to go.

items.question: にほんへ行き ___ です。(I want to go to Japan.)
items.explanation: Verb stem + たい = want to do. 行く → 行き + たい = want to go.

items.question: にほんごをべんきょうし ___ と思っています。(I'm thinking of studying Japanese.)
items.explanation: 〜たいと思っています = am thinking of ~ing. たい + と思っています.

items.question: てがみをおく ___ んですが。(It's that I'd like to send a letter.)
items.explanation: 送る + たい = 送りたい. + んですが adds context/background before a request.

items.question: 〜んですが is used to:
items.answer: Give context before making a request
options: Give context before making a request
Give a reason with から
items.explanation: 〜んですが sets up background information, implying a request follows. Very natural in service situations.

items.question: I don't want to go. Which is correct?
options: 行きたいじゃないです
items.explanation: Negative of たい: たい → たくない + です. 行きたくないです = don't want to go.
